news.title: Samsung's AI smart glasses will be launched in 2026
news.summary: Samsung is preparing to enter the smart glasses market with a camera-equipped model connected to a smartphone for information processing.
news.content: Samsung's upcoming smart glasses will have a built-in camera at 'your eye level' and will be connected to your smartphone to process information received from the camera. This marks Samsung's first foray into the product category, aiming to challenge Meta's Ray-Ban glasses which currently dominate the market.

news.title: The Future of Smart Glasses Was on Full Display at CES 2026
news.summary: CES 2026 showcased an influx of new players and practical applications for smart glasses, indicating a move from early adopter tech to broader consumer category.
news.content: New brands like Amazfit and XGIMI are launching AR glasses thanks to mature technology and supply chains. Practical applications beyond AI include mobile payments, fitness integration, and health tracking. Smart glasses are expected to grow rapidly with new entrants and advancing technology.

news.title: Samsung to Bring AI Features to Older Galaxy Phones
news.summary: Samsung plans to roll out new AI features from its Galaxy S26 series to older smartphones to strengthen the overall performance of Galaxy AI phones against competition.
news.content: Samsung Electronics will deploy its latest smartphone operating system, One UI 8.5, to the Galaxy S25 series, supporting agent features that can perform various app functions through voice commands alone. This move aims to expand Galaxy AI across the entire lineup to boost competitiveness against Apple and Xiaomi.

news.title: Apple's Struggle with Compact iPhones May Impact Foldable Future
news.summary: Apple's difficulty selling compact iPhones like the iPhone mini may affect the development of a clamshell-style foldable iPhone.
news.content: Apple had a 5.4-inch iPhone 12 mini and an iPhone 13 mini, but the device was discontinued after two generations due to poor sales. This struggle with compact models might explain the shelving of a clamshell-style foldable iPhone for now.

news.title: Neurology Group Releases Wearables Guidance
news.summary: The American Association of Neurology released informal guidance regarding the use of wearable devices like smart watches to monitor health and vital signs.
news.content: The guidance suggests potential for wearables to improve patient data quality and quantity, with suggestions including heart rate measurement for seizure clues, sleep detection devices, and movement data for headache disorders. However, the evidence is limited for how applicable device data is in neurology.

news.title: Health Tech Trends at CES 2026
news.summary: CES 2026 highlighted various health tech trends including smart watches with nutrition tracking and AI capabilities to inform people with diabetes about meal impacts.
news.content: Garmin's Connect Plus app now offers nutrition tracking, and Abbott's Libre Assist app feature uses AI to inform diabetics how meals may affect glucose levels. Amazfit's V1tal Food Camera, which monitors eating habits and uploads data to the Zepp app, was also previewed.

news.title: Xreal and Qualcomm Partnership for Smart Glasses
news.summary: Xreal is partnering with Qualcomm to design the operating system, semiconductors, and hardware for mixed-reality technology in smart glasses.
news.content: Xreal has been working with chip designer Qualcomm and Google since 2023 to develop smart glasses that combine augmented and virtual reality. The partnership aims to create a new era of mobile agentic AI, with the first product being the Galaxy XR headset based on Google's Android 'XR' operating system.

news.title: Apple's Retreat from Smart Glasses Market
news.summary: Apple has paused development of a revamped Vision Pro and is redirecting resources toward smart glasses similar to Meta's Ray-Bans.
news.content: Apple's manufacturing partner Luxshare halted Vision Pro production in early 2025, and the company is now focusing on basic smart glasses without a display, projected to launch in 2027. The retreat is attributed to high pricing, discomfort during extended use, and lack of an app ecosystem.

news.title: Zephyr RTOS Adds New Security Features
news.summary: The Zephyr project has released a new version of its RTOS with enhanced security features, aiming to provide a more secure environment for IoT devices.
news.content: The latest version of Zephyr RTOS introduces security enhancements that include protection against common vulnerabilities and improved isolation mechanisms. These updates are crucial for ensuring the reliability and safety of IoT devices powered by Zephyr.

news.title: RT-Thread Partners with STM32 for IoT Development
news.summary: RT-Thread has announced a partnership with STM32 to facilitate IoT development, offering a comprehensive software platform for microcontroller-based applications.
news.content: RT-Thread's collaboration with STM32 provides developers with an integrated solution that combines RT-Thread's real-time operating system with STM32's microcontroller technology, simplifying the development process for IoT devices.

news.title: Turing AI Updates Its Platform with New APIs
news.summary: Turing AI has released an update to its platform, introducing new APIs that allow for more flexible and efficient integration with third-party services.
news.content: The update to Turing AI's platform includes the addition of new APIs that enable developers to integrate Turing's AI capabilities into their applications more seamlessly. This move is part of Turing's strategy to expand its AI ecosystem and enhance user experience.

news.title: 涂鸦智能 Announces Global Expansion Plans
news.summary: 涂鸦智能 has announced plans for global expansion, aiming to increase its market presence and provide IoT solutions to a wider audience.
news.content: 涂鸦智能's expansion plans include entering new markets and offering localized IoT solutions. The company's focus on smart home and IoT technologies positions it to capitalize on the growing global demand for connected devices.

news.title: New Advancements in Quantum Computing
news.summary: Recent developments in quantum computing have the potential to revolutionize the field, offering significant improvements in processing power and efficiency.
news.content: Researchers have made significant strides in quantum computing, with new advancements that could lead to more powerful and efficient quantum computers. These developments are expected to have a profound impact on various industries, including AI and cryptography.

news.title: Breakthrough in Flexible Electronics for Wearable Devices
news.summary: Scientists have achieved a breakthrough in flexible electronics, which could lead to the development of more comfortable and functional wearable devices.
news.content: The new flexible electronics technology allows for the creation of wearable devices that can bend and flex with the body without losing functionality. This breakthrough is expected to enhance the user experience and open up new possibilities for wearable tech.
